feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Concur
feed.subtitle: SAP Concur blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Streamlining List Management in SAP Concur with Real-Time API Integration
entry.content:
Introduction

As a Technical Consultant at SAP Concur, I’ve helped many clients streamline their integrations. One recurring challenge? Keeping lists accurate and up-to-date. That’s where our new List Management Use Case comes in.

Managing dynamic lists—such as departments, project codes, or cost centers—is an important part of ensuring accurate data entry and enforcing business rules in SAP Concur.

The Challenge: Keeping Lists Accurate and Up-to-Date

In many organizations, the system of record for lists resides outside of Concur. Whether it’s a fast-growing company with frequent organizational changes or a global enterprise managing complex hierarchies, the need for real-time list synchronization is clear. Manual updates via the UI or Excel templates are time-consuming and error-prone, and overnight batch jobs can’t keep pace with dynamic environments.

The Solution: SAP Concur List & List Item APIs

This use case introduces a robust, API-driven approach to list management. By leveraging SAP Concur’s List v4 and List Item v4 APIs, organizations can:

  • Retrieve lists and list items
  • Create new items, including multi-level list items
  • Update existing list items
  • Delete outdated or incorrect items

All of this can be done programmatically, enabling real-time updates and seamless integration with external systems.

Key Benefits

Implementing this solution delivers tangible benefits:

  • Efficiency: Reduces manual effort and streamlines processes
  • Accuracy: Ensures consistent, up-to-date data across platforms
  • Scalability: Supports bulk operations and complex hierarchies
  • User Experience: Improves usability by presenting relevant, current options

Best Practices and Resources

The documentation also includes a detailed walkthrough, API sequences, performance guidelines, and error handling tips.
Use Case -List Management

For developers, a ready-to-use Postman collection is available on GitHub, making implementation faster and easier.
GitHub - SAP-samples/concur-web-services-list

Conclusion

Real-time list management with SAP Concur APIs offers a scalable, efficient way to keep your data accurate and aligned across systems. It reduces manual effort, simplifies integration, and improves the experience for both users and administrators.

entry.title: How SAP and PETRONAS Are Fueling the Future of Performance
entry.content:
Driving Efficiency and Innovation.

In Formula 1, every millisecond counts — and behind every lap of precision lies a world of data, integration, and intelligent technology. The same principles drive the partnership between SAP and PETRONAS, extending far beyond the racetrack.

In its 50th anniversary year, PETRONAS – the dynamic global energy group and long-time partner of the Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One Team – continues to accelerate its digital transformation journey. One of the most significant milestones in this process has been its strategic partnership with SAP, through the adoption of SAP Concur for travel and expense management.

For years, PETRONAS relied on SAP’s traditional SAP Concur Travel & Expense (TVM) solution. However, as the platform reached its end of life, the company faced a familiar challenge: excessive customization costs and increasing complexity. This was the perfect moment to rethink processes and shift towards a more integrated and intelligent approach.

A Seamless Move to SAP Concur

Transitioning to SAP Concur was not just a system migration – it was a business transformation. The move allowed PETRONAS to align travel and expense operations with the broader SAP ecosystem, which already serves as the organization's digital backbone.

With SAP Concur, employees now benefit from end-to-end visibility, covering every step from travel request to expense reporting. The system brings cost transparency, allowing users to clearly see flight, accommodation, and meal allowance options in real time.

Since its full rollout in 2022, PETRONAS has reported a 20% improvement in travel cost efficiency. This measurable gain highlights how digital integration can directly contribute to financial discipline and operational excellence.

Beyond Efficiency: Building the Future Together

But this partnership is not just about cost savings. PETRONAS and SAP are working together to co-innovate and future-proof business processes. The next frontier involves leveraging AI, particularly generative AI, to minimize manual intervention and automate monitoring, freeing finance teams to focus on strategic value creation.

As PETRONAS continues to push the limits of technology both on and off the racetrack, the collaboration with SAP reflects a shared mindset: continuous innovation, transparency, and performance.

Just as precision and data drive the Formula 1 team’s success, the same principles now power PETRONAS’ financial operations — ensuring that every digital pit stop contributes to a faster, smarter, and more sustainable future.

The São Paulo Grand Prix: Innovation Meets Performance in Brazil

This weekend, attention turns to the Formula 1 MSC Cruises Grande Prémio de São Paulo, taking place at the iconic Autódromo José Carlos Pace (Interlagos) in Brazil. The event showcases not only speed and competition, but the spirit of innovation and performance that also underpins PETRONAS and SAP’s collaboration. (f1saopaulo.com.br)

From high-octane racing to high-impact business transformation, the connection is clear: whether on the track or in the boardroom, excellence and technology go hand in hand.

From racetrack to boardroom, innovation is a team sport. SAP and PETRONAS demonstrate how technology and vision can drive performance at every level — turning data into decisions, and processes into progress.

Today, technology creates champions.
And as the largest European technology company, SAP empowers winners in sports, in business, and in your organization.

SAP Concur

entry.title: Kick Off 2026 With Impact: Collaborate on 56 Fresh SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Projects
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that Cycle 1 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is now live!
This cycle features 56 new projects, offering SAP customers and partners the opportunity to gain early insights into upcoming product developments and collaborate directly with SAP teams to shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

🗓Registration is open until March 13, 2026.

In a rapidly evolving business environment, early involvement can make a meaningful difference. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can work directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing your feedback on concepts, prototypes, and plans while they are still in the early stages.

To help you explore all available opportunities, we’ve prepared a complete overview of the projects.
View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the topics most relevant to you.

SAP Ariba

  • Reporting and Analytics in SAP Ariba Invoicing

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-powered Code Upgrade Automation
  • BPTX Early Insights
  • Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users - provide feedback on new and planned features
  • SAP BTP Developer's Guide
  • SAP BTP: Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience
  • SAP Integration Suite
  • Validate MCP Hub

SAP Concur

  • Intelligent Audit Evolution: Accelerating Audits with Automation and AI
  • New User Onboarding Experience in SAP Concur
  • Reimagining Travel & Expense with SAP Concur-embedded virtual cards experience
  • SAP Concur - User Profile Management
  • SAP Concur Expense: Streamlining Corporate Card Administration
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) - Data Platform
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) - Recommendations
  • Trip Extras - Connecting suppliers to business travelers when it matters most.
  • Verify: Shaping the Future of Auditing and Compliance

Data & Analytics

  • Exploring BDC Web & Mobile Experiences to drive user relevancy and adoption
  • Product Insights / Product 360
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • A modern Output Management experience in ERP Suite
  • Agentic AI - Analytical Business Insights  
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI Agent with Financial Correspondences
  • AI Assisted SAP Workflow Management
  • AI assisted use cases for Embedded Analytics
  • AI-assisted task automation
  • AI in Core Logistics (HUM, SN, BM, MM-IM, Kanban)
  • Business Partner and Product Master in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Concept for Harmonized Period Control
  • Configuration experience of Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Customer returns user experience
  • E-mails in business context with integrated business communications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Embedded AI and Joule for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Enhanced Payment Order Management in Automatic Payment Run Process
  • Farmer Collaboration and Engagement
  • Generate Settlement Rules
  • Identity and Access Management: Upgrade and Change Handling
  • IFRS 9- Credit-Risk Based Impairment for Trade Receivables using Advanced Valuation
  • Improving Sanction Party List screening with AI
  • Improving the Usability of Task and Application Logs in Group Reporting
  • Limit Management Modernization (Credit Risk Analyzer)
  • Material Long Texts – Automated Content Generation for Product Master
  • Parallel Unit of Measure
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies & provide smart staffing across different roles of projects
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Simplify and Improve the Intrastat Declaration Process with AI
  • Task Center (One Inbox) experience evaluation in SAP Business Suite
  • Understanding Payment Reconciliation Processes and Automation Barriers in Bank Statements
  • Unlock more flexible Margin Analysis in SAP Cloud ERP

SAP Globalization

  • AI Agents for SAP Translation Hub
  • AI Assisted E-Document Error Handling : Continuous Adaptation

Others

  • Agentic AI Operations with SAP Automation Pilot
  • How Designers and Developers Use SAP Design System Resources
  • Integrated Planning in Dairy
  • Research into an Intelligent Spare Parts Request Processing Agent
  • SAP Reference Architecture

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Shape the Future of SAP Concur with SAP Customer Engagement Initiative: Cycle 1 - 2026
entry.content:
It’s time for something new in travel and expense

No one connects expense, travel, and invoice on a single platform like SAP Concur.

Connect financial data and take control of spending. By automating spending processes your business can run efficiently anywhere, anytime, and under any circumstances.

 

SAP Concur is constantly evolving, and you have the opportunity to shape its future. Scroll through the list of SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects for SAP Concur and get a sneak peek at the planned new features and functions.

 

Scroll through the list of SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects for SAP Concur and get informed about the planned new features and functions.

And don’t forget to register for one of the projects until March 13, 2026

SAP Concur Solutions projects for registration:

 

  • Intelligent Audit Evolution: Accelerating Audits with Automation and AI
  • New User Onboarding Experience in SAP Concur
  • Reimagining Travel & Expense with SAP Concur-embedded virtual cards experience
  • SAP Concur Expense: Streamlining Corporate Card Administration
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) – Data Platform
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) – Recommendations
  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/4173
  • Trip Extras – Connecting suppliers to business travelers when it matters most
  • Verify: Shaping the Future of Auditing and Compliance

 

 

Visit the Customer Influence website on influence.sap.com to register.

If you are also interested in other CEI projects that are currently open for registration, see here the full list.

Thank you in advance for your participation!

 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: SAP Concur Payroll Integration via ICS
entry.content:
The Payroll Integration allows clients to transfer payroll data to their SAP Payroll system as a method for reimbursing their employees for approved business expenses.

SAP Concur ICS (Integration with Concur Solutions) enables seamless, automated data flow between SAP Concur Expense and SAP ERP systems like SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC.

ICS supports payroll integration by enabling expense reimbursement data to flow directly from Concur to SAP payroll systems.

Payroll Integration helps to reduce manual work by automatically transferring expense financial posting data to the SAP Payroll system.

Once an expense report is successfully posted in SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC system, it will automatically send the report to SAP Payroll system for processing, allowing companies to reimburse out of pocket expenses and accurately assign wage types and tax related details. ‑of‑pocket expenses and accurately assign wage types and tax‑related details.

A payroll document is created only after its related expense report is fully posted, ensuring accuracy and consistency in payroll and financial data.

Systems Involved:

  • SAP Concur – where employees create and submit expense reports
  • SAP FI – validates and posts the expense (no payment yet)
  • SAP HCM / EC Payroll – stores expense data as HR master data
  • SAP Payroll– calculates tax and pays the employee via payroll

System Landscape Options:

Payroll integration via ICS works with:

  • SAP FI + SAP HCM in the same system
  • SAP FI and HCM in separate systems
  • SAP FI in cloud + HCM on‑premise
  • EC Payroll (SuccessFactors)

1. Configure Payroll Integration in SAP Concur

Step 1: Login to Concur and navigate to Administration → Expense → Group Configuration

Select the respective group and Tick (✓) Enable Financial Integration setting and then Tick (✓) Enable Payroll Integration setting.

Once these settings are enabled, concur admin can be able to see the “Payroll Integration” setting under Company → Tools → Payroll Integration

Step 2: Under “Payroll Settings” select the expense group for which payroll Integration is activated. Tick (✓) the check box to use the payroll integration to reimburse employees belong to this group. Once this setting is enabled, all expenses will be paid through payroll.

Enter the below clearing External wage type codes that are maintained in HR/HCM system

  • Cash Advance Clearing Wage Type: This code is used to deduct the cash advances from the employee’s payable amount.
  • Personal Expense Clearing Wage Type: This code is used to deduct the employee’s pay by the amount they spent for personal use on the company card.
  • Payroll Clearing Account Code: This code is used to clear the payroll liability after the amount has been paid to the employee.
  • Benefits in Kind Wage Type: This code is used to record amounts that are treated as a taxable benefit for the employee.

Fill in the Default Wage Types fields. These wage types will be applied when no expense‑specific wage type has been defined on the Wage Types tab.

Once all the required details are entered, click on “Save” button to save the payroll settings.

Step 3: Navigate to “Wage Types” tab and map the wage type codes to the expenses maintained in SAP HR/HCM. This overwrites the default wage type mapping maintained in the Payroll Settings “Default Wage Types” settings.

Once all the wage types are mapped to the expenses, click on “Save” button to save the wage type mappings.

Note: If “Enable Payroll Reimbursement” checkbox is enabled then all the expenses will be transferred to SAP Payroll even if “Transfer Amounts to Payroll?” flag is not selected in the “Wage Types” tab.

To use Payroll for reporting only, then deactivate "Enable Payroll Reimbursement" setting under “Payroll Settings” tab.

If this setting is disabled, then we need to select the expense types that should be transferred to payroll only for reporting/taxation.

Navigate to “Wage Types” tab and select the respective expense types that should be sent only for reporting/taxation. Tick (✓) "Transfer Amounts to Payroll" Check Box for selected expense types. Click on “Save” to save the Wage Types settings. These expenses will be reimbursed via Accounts Payable but not via Payroll.

Note: Taxable travel allowances always flow to payroll.

Step 4: Configure Payroll Wizard in SAP Concur ICS

  • Login to SAP ECC or S/4 HANA system and execute the T-Code: “CTE_SETUP”
  • Open “Integration Activities” and enable “Payroll” connection
  • Configure “Payroll” ICS wizard with required mappings according to client requirements for payroll posting

 

2. Expense Report Payroll Process

  High Level Architecture Diagram:

Process Flow: SAP Concur → SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA (FI & Payroll Postings) → SAP Concur → SAP HCM → Payroll Run → Employee paid via payroll

Step 2.1: SAP Concur – Expense Report Process (Top Blue Box from above process diagram)

  • Employee creates and submits an expense report in SAP Concur (E.g., Breakfast, Taxi, hotel)
  • Review & fully approve the expense report by respective approvers & Processors.
  • SAP Concur sends all the fully approved expense reports which are in payment status “Processing Payment” to SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA (This is mandatory, even for payroll reimbursement)

Step 2.2: SAP Concur Expense and Payroll Posting in SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA via ICS (Yellow Box from above process diagram)

  • SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA automatically imports Expense reports into ICS wizard under Financial Postings → Concur Documents based on the scheduled import job.
  • SAP ICS validates the imported reports and verifies the associated cost objects, G/L accounts, and company codes.
  • Once all the expense data is processed without any errors, then expense documents are posted to SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA (on Payroll Clearing Account) successfully.
  • For Out-of-Pocket‑of‑Pocket expenses, SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA doesn’t issue a direct payment to the employee or vendor. Instead, it posts the amount to a Payroll Clearing GL account which is maintained in “Payroll Settings” in SAP Concur, which allows the payroll team to reimburse the employee through their regular salary process.
  • SAP ICS sends success or failure status back to Concur
    • If Expense posting is failed → process ends here and employee/processor needs to correct and resubmit expense report in Concur
    • If Expense Posting is successful → Payroll process will continue

Important rule: No FI posting = No Payroll Posting

  • Once the FI posting is successful, SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA automatically imports successfully posted expense reports into Payroll ICS wizard for payroll posting based on the scheduled import job.

Step 2.3: Transfer of expense data to HCM via Concur

  • Once FI and Payroll posting is successful, Concur automatically triggers the HCM and sends the expense data from Concur to SAP HCM
  • Below details are transferred to HCM:
  • Employee ID
  • Amount
  • Expense types
  • Mapped wage types

Step 2.4: SAP HCM Master Data Setup for Concur Payroll Postings (Green Box from above process diagram)

 Import Reports

  • SAP HCM receives payroll-relevant expense data

Create Info Type 0579

  • Data is written into Info Type 0579
  • Info Type 0579 = External Wage Type Components

Each expense line item becomes:

  • A wage type
  • With an amount
  • Assigned to the employee
  • Stored temporarily for payroll

Key point:  Info Type 0579 is a staging area, but no employee payment yet 

Step 2.5:  Payroll Process

 Execute Payroll

Payroll program run for the payroll period and calculates the amount to be reimbursed for the employee after the necessary deductions. Employees can be able to see net reimbursement and deductions in their salary pay slip.

Note: Payment is made via Standard payroll bank transfer

Step 2.6: Concur Audit Trail update

Once the payroll posting is successful, then the status of the report will be automatically updated in Audit trail in Concur.

To view the Audit trail, select the respective report and go to → Details → Audit Trail to track the status in the expense report. 

3. Monitor Concur Payroll Posting Document Failures

Login to Concur and navigate to Company→ Tools → Payroll Integration → Posting failures tab. Under this tab, concur admin can be able to see all the failure posting documents.

Concur Admins can search for failed postings using the Report ID, Report Name, Employee ID, First Name, or Last Name.

By selecting a checkbox next to an expense report, admin can retry the payroll transfer by clicking on “Retry Payroll Transfer button” at the bottom of the screen.

4. Monitor Concur Payroll Document status

Under “Document Status” tab, admin can be able to search and check the status of the expense reports that are in “Success/Acknowledged/Ready”

5. General FAQ’s

Q1: What types of expenses are sent to Payroll?

A: Only out-of-pocket (cash) expenses—those paid personally by the employee—are sent to payroll for reimbursement. Corporate card transactions typically do not post through payroll unless any expense types selected for tax/reporting purpose. ‑of‑pocket (cash) expenses—those paid personally by the employee—are sent to payroll for reimbursement. Corporate card transactions typically do not

Q2: How are personal expenses handled by Payroll?

A: Any personal transactions charged to a company paid card are recorded under the Personal Expense Clearing wage type and deducted from the employee’s total payroll reimbursement. 

Q3: What are the key limitations associated with Payroll Integration?

A:

1) Partial employee payments in FI and HR are not supported and require customer‑specific enhancements, such as implementing a BADI in ECC or SAP S/4HANA On‑Premise.

2) Cash advance cannot be supported through Payroll Integration, as the feature does not support paying or posting cash advances

3) Payroll Integration will not support payments for IBCP, CBCP, or other company‑paid card transactions, since these are not intended to flow through payroll.

entry.title: Curious about SAP’s latest path to Concur request certification?
entry.content:
We are excited to share that SAP Learning has released a brand‑new Learning Journey Becoming a Certified Configuration Administrator in Concur Request Professional Edition  designed for everyone who works with in an administration or configuration role.

This offering provides a complete, role‑based path to help you:

  • Understand the foundational features of Concur Request
  • Create, manage, and approve request workflows
  • Apply travel segments and expected expenses within the system
  • Build and optimize approval workflows
  • Use the configuration and administration tools necessary to effectively manage Concur Request environments 

This journey aligns perfectly with administrators seeking to deepen their expertise and build the skills needed for certification.

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • Concur Request Administrators
  • Configuration Specialists
  • Implementation or Support Consultants
  • Anyone aiming to become certified on Concur Request Professional Edition

If you have prior experience using Concur Request as a business user, this journey will help you take the next step toward expert-level configuration and administration.

 Start learning today:  Access the full journey free of charge on SAP Learning: 👉 here

entry.title: Ready to become the go‑to expert for Concur Expense?
entry.content:
A brand‑new SAP Learning Journey is here—Becoming a Certified Configuration Consultant in Concur Expense, Professional Edition—designed for anyone aiming to build deep expertise in configuring SAP Concur Expense and validating their skills with certification.

This guided, role‑based journey walks you through everything from foundational product knowledge to advanced configuration scenarios. Whether you're new to SAP Concur or looking to formalize your experience, the path combines hands‑on practice, expert‑led content, and structured learning steps that prepare you thoroughly for the certification exam.

Prerequisites

  • CE0001: Getting Started with Concur Expense for Business Users 
  • CLS01: Getting Started with Support from SAP (Support Accreditation) 

Explore real‑world configuration tasks, learn best practices, and build the confidence needed to implement and manage SAP Concur Expense solutions effectively in customer environments.

Earn your badge 
Prove your skills and earn a certification that validates your expertise: SAP CERTIFICATION • C_CE325 SAP Certified - Configuration Consultant - Concur Expense Professional Edition 

👉Start your journey now: here

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter March 2026
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here a preview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Getting Started with the New SAP Concur Travel for business users

Discover how the new SAP Concur Travel makes planning and booking work trips easier while helping you stay aligned with your company’s travel program. In just a short read, see what’s changing, how it supports your travelers, and how to get enabled along the way.

Full blog

Ready to Upgrade SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition?

Don’t let myths hold you back! Many teams hesitate to upgrade SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition due to common misconceptions—like being "too many releases behind" or fearing major disruptions. But what if upgrades could be seamless, strategic, and even empower your business?

Learn more

Meet our experts from SAP Enterprise Support to get updates

Join our session on March 25 to meet our experts from SAP Enterprise Support Academy and SAP Enterprise Support Advisory program. Get latest insights about the move to learning.sap.com and learning highlights and hear which workstream the ESAC innovation program offers in Q1.

Stay tuned and join on You Tube

See how SAP is transforming Ariba into an AI‑native source‑to‑pay platform

SAP is reimagining Ariba as an AI‑native, modern source‑to‑pay platform designed to simplify daily work, improve decision-making, and streamline procurement-to-pay processes. Read the blog to see practical examples, product highlights, and what these changes mean for faster cycle times, stronger cost control, and lower risk across your organization.

Read the full blog

New SAP for Me Services & Support dashboard-now live

We’ve launched a redesigned, user‑centric Services & Support overview tab to make support faster and easier. Enjoy a prominent search, actionable cards for Manage Cases, Quick Access (CIC), service requests and recommendations, unified case management, and simplified navigation — all designed to speed resolutions, boost self‑service, and improve outcomes.

Explore the new dashboard and share your feedback

Heads up for the intelligent front door-one digital experience in SAP for Me

SAP’s new unified entry point in SAP for Me will bring conversational and agentic AI to your fingertips. Discover how effortless navigation, advanced self-service, personalized guidance, and lightning-fast task execution will simplify daily workflows and speed decision-making across your SAP landscape. 

Stay tuned for the launch in May. More details: Intelligent Front Door - Info sheet

Introducing CQC Security Optimization for SAP SuccessFactors-Now in pilot!

Protecting the security and availability of your SAP SuccessFactors environment is vital to keeping your business running smoothly. With the CQC Security Optimization service, you gain proactive analysis and insights to help prevent critical issues before they arise.

Learn more

Your starting point for practical SAP enablement

SAP Enterprise Support Academy is now on learning.sap.com, bringing together hands-on learning, expert guidance, practical resources, and expert-led live sessions that sessions that support you, so you can move from planning to action more quickly.

Learn more here

ALM Summit NA - April 14-16 in Newtown Square-March

The landscape of Application Lifecycle Management is transforming, and you can witness this evolution firsthand at the SAP ALM Summit North America 2026.

Dates: April 14–16, 2026
Location: SAP US Headquarters, Newtown Square, PA

Register here

Customer Success Through Trusted Partner Support Expertise

SAP Cloud customers rely on implementation and operations services from SAP’s partner ecosystem, where an effective support collaboration between partner and SAP is essential for Customer Success.  To unlock incremental value, such as faster issue resolution and risk mitigation, customers are encouraged to select and manage partners also based on support quality.

Read the article and learn more

Related links:

  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy on LSC (learning.sap.com)
  • SAP Support Portal
  • SAP Enterprise Support value maps 
  • Video

entry.title: Looking to level up your SAP Concur skills and strengthen your admin superpowers?
entry.content:
We’ve got something tailor‑made for you! Our brand‑new Learning Journey is now live:
👉 Managing Company Administration in Legacy Concur Travel

You will 

  • Build confident, hands‑on expertise in key admin tasks
  • Understand how to efficiently manage travel policies, configurations & workflows
  • Get step‑by‑step guidance that’s practical, clear, and ready to use
  • Learn at your own speed — and revisit anytime 💡

Who is it for?
Anyone who touches Concur Travel administration: new admins, transitioning roles, or anyone looking to brush up on the essentials.

 Ready to grow your skills? 
Dive in, explore, and take your Concur Travel knowledge to the next level.
Your next skill boost starts with one click!

entry.title: Level Up Your Career: How to Get Certified as a Concur Travel Configuration Consultant
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Becoming a Certified Configuration Consultant in legacy Concur Travel Professional Edition learning journey.

Learning outcome
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to:

Navigate and implement legacy Concur Travel. You will manage the main features and functionalities of both administrative areas of the tool, understand their complexity and relationship, and be able to configure a Concur Travel site based on the customer requirements.

 

Goals
  • Get started with SAP: Building the basics
  • Develop your expertise: Skill deepening learning
  • Excel in your expertise: Advanced specialization learning
 
Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! 
We are here to support you.


I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards, Lian

entry.title: 🚀 SAP Mini Inside Track – Chennai | Coming April 2026
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that the SAP Chennai Community is gearing up to host an upcoming SAP Mini Inside Track (SIT) in April 2026! 🎉

This community‑driven event will bring together SAP professionals, consultants, developers, architects, students, and enthusiasts from across Chennai and nearby regions to collaborate, learn, and share knowledge.

📍 Date & Venue:
Details will be announced very soon — stay tuned!

🌟 What to Expect at SAP Mini Inside Track – Chennai

✅ Cutting‑Edge SAP Topics
Explore insightful sessions covering the latest in SAP technologies, innovations, and real‑world experiences — including S/4HANA, BTP, integrations, cloud, AI, and more.

✅ Knowledge Sharing by the Community
Learn from local SAP experts and practitioners across various organizations who will share practical use cases, lessons learned, and best practices.

✅ Networking & Collaboration
Connect with fellow SAP professionals, exchange ideas, expand your network, and be part of meaningful conversations within the SAP ecosystem.

✅ Open Community Platform
Bring your thoughts, questions, and experiences — this is a collaborative space built by the community, for the community.

🔔 Stay Connected

More information on event agenda, speakers, registration, date, and venue will be shared shortly through this page.

Follow the SAP Chennai Community Page and keep an eye out for updates — exciting times ahead! 🙌

Let’s come together to learn, connect, and grow the SAP ecosystem in Chennai.

#SAPMiniInsideTrack #SAPChennai #SAPCommunity #SAPEvents
#InsideTrack #SAPLearning #SAPNetworking #ChennaiTech #SAPProfessionals

 

Just say the word 👍

Regards

Prasath

entry.title: Inside SAP IT 2026: Real Stories, Real Lessons, Real SAP – Live on YouTube
entry.content:
SAP runs SAP

It’s more than a slogan—it’s a commitment. SAP’s own IT organization (CPIT) acts as Customer Zero, adopting and validating SAP innovations before they reach the market. This means every new capability, every architectural shift, and every AI breakthrough is tested first inside one of the world’s most complex enterprise landscapes.

In 2026, we’re taking you behind the scenes again with a new season of Inside SAP IT, our live YouTube series where SAP experts share unfiltered insights, lessons learned, and the real stories behind SAP’s internal transformation.

Authentic. Practical. Unscripted.

 

Whether you’re an IT leader, architect, developer, or business transformation professional, this series gives you a front‑row seat to how SAP builds, scales, and operates the technologies shaping the intelligent, agentic enterprise.

⭐ What to expect from this season

Across ten episodes, SAP IT leaders and experts will unpack how SAP is:

  • Running agentic AI at enterprise scale
  • Modernizing core processes with SAP S/4HANA, SAP BTP, SAP Sales Cloud, SAP CPQ, and SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Operationalizing data products in SAP Business Data Cloud
  • Reinventing support, finance, HR, and revenue operations with AI agents
  • Delivering intelligent experiences through SAP for Me and SAP’s internal platforms
  • Transforming planning, treasury, travel & expense, and more

Each session is live, interactive, and openly shares what worked, what didn’t, and what others can learn from SAP’s journey.

 📅Inside SAP IT – 2026 Live Series Schedule

Episode 1


 

 

 

 

Date: Thu, Apr 23 – 15:00 CEST

SAP opens the 2026 season with a rare inside look at how one of the world’s most complex IT landscapes is run, evolved, and continuously reinvented. Hear directly from SAP’s CPIO on what it means to act as Customer Zero, how agentic AI is reshaping work, and what keeps SAP’s IT leadership awake at night.

Why join:

  • Learn how SAP navigates complexity at global scale
  • Understand where value shifts as automation accelerates
  • Hear what skills tomorrow’s IT leaders need now

Speakers: 

Dr. Benjamin Blau, Chief Process & Information Officer
Dr. Oliver Gutzeit, Leader Experience Technology

Host: Tania Dinnendahl - Executive Liaison Manager, SAP Global User Groups Organization

👉Register: events.sap.com/sap-runs-sap

🔎YouTube Live: youtube.com/live/0XiObKbSYhA - (Use the same link to watch the session at your convenience)

⏰Add to calendar: community.sap.com/khhcw49343/downloadCalendar?message.id=14361879

 

Episode 2

 

 

 

 

 

Date: Tue, Jun 16 – 15:00 CEST

SAP for Me is becoming the intelligent front door for all your SAP interactions. Discover how AI guidance and a unified task center make it easier to find answers, manage services, and get things done faster.

Why join:

  • See the latest innovations in SAP for Me
  • Learn how AI improves navigation and support
  • Get a look at SAP’s internal adoption journey

Speaker: Anna Withum, Customer Collaboration Manager of SAP for Me

Host: Tania Dinnendahl - Executive Liaison Manager, SAP Global User Groups Organization

👉Register: events.sap.com/sap-runs-sap

🔎YouTube Live: youtube.com/live/8NvZ8D8mSAw - (Use the same link to watch the session at your convenience)

 

Episode 3

 

 

 

 

 

Date: Thu, Jun 25 – 15:00 CEST

Explore how SAP operationalizes data products in SAP Business Data Cloud to accelerate insights and scale AI-driven innovation. See real internal examples of how SAP makes the AI–Data–Apps flywheel spin.

Why join:

  • Learn how SAP builds and governs data products
  • See how AI and human intelligence reinforce each other
  • Understand how SAP removes IT bottlenecks

Speaker: Oliver Huth, Head of Platform - Enterprise Analytics

Host: Tania Dinnendahl - Executive Liaison Manager, SAP Global User Groups Organization

👉Register: events.sap.com/sap-runs-sap

(Link will follow)

 

Episode 4

 

 

 

 

 

Date: Tue, Jun 30 – 15:00 CEST

Becoming AI-first requires new thinking, fast experimentation, and a clear operating model. SAP shares its top five lessons learned, including the Enterprise AI Operating Model and the agentic AI Golden Path.

Why join:

  • Learn what truly accelerates AI adoption
  • Understand SAP’s internal AI governance
  • See how SAP builds enterprise-grade AI agents

Speaker: Majeed Malik, Lead of Enterprise AI Product & Partner Management

Host: Tania Dinnendahl - Executive Liaison Manager, SAP Global User Groups Organization

👉Register: events.sap.com/sap-runs-sap

🔎YouTube Live: youtube.com/live/f5QdxEi7Hq4 - (Use the same link to watch the session at your convenience)

 

Episode 5

 

 

 

 

 

Date: Tue, Jul 16 – 15:00 CEST

SAP deployed Joule’s Cash Management Agent to automate bank statements, suggest cash movements, and unify treasury processes. Get a behind-the-scenes look at how Customer Zero built an enterprise-ready agentic finance architecture.

Why join:

  • See real AI agent use cases in finance
  • Learn how SAP achieved measurable efficiency gains
  • Understand the architecture behind Agentic Finance

Speakers: 

Waldemar Kessel, Head of Intelligent Real Estate & Treasury Solutions (IRES & TR) at SAP IT
Damian Preisner, Head of Finance Process&Solutions Excellence (FPSE)

Host: Tania Dinnendahl - Executive Liaison Manager, SAP Global User Groups Organization

👉Registration: events.sap.com/sap-runs-sap

🔎YouTube Live: youtube.com/live/O5gRhN3g0sc - (Use the same link to watch the session at your convenience)

 

Episode 6

 

 

 

 

 

Date: Thu, Jul 23 – 15:00 CEST

Discover how SAP uses AI-enabled HR technology to support a skills-led organization. Learn how AI agents enhance HR processes, talent management, and strategic workforce planning.

Why join:

  • See how AI transforms HR operations
  • Learn how SAP builds a skills-led organization
  • Explore real agentic HR use cases

 

Speakers: 

Georg Arnold, IT Senior Manager
Karl Baert, Global Head of People Solutions

Host: Tania Dinnendahl - Executive Liaison Manager, SAP Global User Groups Organization

👉Registration: events.sap.com/sap-runs-sap

🔎YouTube Live: youtube.com/live/34EH4bpFziQ - (Use the same link to watch the session at your convenience)

 

Episode 7

 

 

 

 

 

Date: Thu, Sept 08 – 15:00 CEST

SAP recently rolled out Concur Travel & Expense AI solutions, including Joule-powered agents. Hear the internal story from both business and IT perspectives.

Why join:

  • Learn how SAP modernized T&E with AI
  • Understand rollout strategy and logistics
  • Get insights into SAP’s internal adoption

Speaker: Jürgen Dresler, IT Travel & Expense Chief Product Owner at SAP IT

Host: Tania Dinnendahl - Executive Liaison Manager, SAP Global User Groups Organization

👉Registration: events.sap.com/sap-runs-sap

🔎YouTube Live: youtube.com/live/xSgqO5wUGKk - (Use the same link to watch the session at your convenience)

 

Episode 8

 

 

 

 

 

Date: Tue, Sept 15 – 15:00 CEST

SAP is shifting from reactive support to anticipatory operations using policy-governed agents. See how agents enrich cases, execute fixes, and improve reliability at scale.

Why join:

  • Learn how SAP “agentifies” operations
  • Understand proactive support models
  • See real examples from SAP’s cloud operations

Speaker: Dr. Michele Weynandt, Head of Product Management for Service Management Processes and Solutions

Host: Tania Dinnendahl - Executive Liaison Manager, SAP Global User Groups Organization

👉Registration: events.sap.com/sap-runs-sap

🔎YouTube Live: youtube.com/live/T0fIvLFIH8s - (Use the same link to watch the session at your convenience)

 

Episode 9

 

 

 

 

 

Date: Thu, Oct 15 – 15:00 CEST

SAP is modernizing its revenue operations architecture to build a clean, unified foundation for agentic AI across quoting, contracting, and provisioning.

Why join:

  • Learn how SAP simplifies complex revenue processes
  • See how SAP Sales Cloud & SAP CPQ work together
  • Understand the architecture behind intelligent RevOps

Speaker: Marius Weigand, VP of Commercial Process and Technology Transformation, Customer & Partner Processes & IT

Host: Tania Dinnendahl - Executive Liaison Manager, SAP Global User Groups Organization

👉Registration: events.sap.com/sap-runs-sap

 🔎YouTube Live: youtube.com/live/_0VarQt4oYM - (Use the same link to watch the session at your convenience)

 

Episode 10

 

 

 

 

 

Date: Tue, Oct 20 – 15:00 CEST

SAP has fully transitioned its financial and cross-functional planning to SAP Analytics Cloud. Learn how SAP built an integrated, agentic, data-product-driven planning platform.

Why join:

  • See SAP’s internal xP&A strategy
  • Learn from real planning use cases
  • Understand lessons learned from the transformation

Speaker: Daniel Lauer, Head of Planning Engineering

Host: Tania Dinnendahl - Executive Liaison Manager, SAP Global User Groups Organization

👉Registration: events.sap.com/sap-runs-sap

🔎YouTube Live: youtube.com/live/8NvZ8D8mSAw

 

Join Us Live – Learn from SAP as Customer Zero

Inside SAP IT is your chance to see how SAP applies its own technology, solves real enterprise challenges, and builds the foundation for the next generation of agentic, intelligent operations.

Follow the series, join the conversations, and bring these insights back to your own organization.

Click here to register and be informed!!

entry.title: Which Business AI solutions are available within my SAP Cloud Subscription?
entry.content:
In the last couple of months, I have been talking to many SAP customers looking to implement AI initiatives in their companies to improve productivity gains and/or because their management is asking about AI in their current business processes.

At the same time, generative AI models have made it easy for a set of SAP business end users to start prompting in natural language and create supplemental ideas for their day-to-day work, adding speed and pressure of AI to their IT counterparts or simply ignoring IT and creating their own tool domains.

However, we all know that while the accessibility of AI tools to SAP business end users is proliferating for idea creation, creating ideas via prompts doesn’t address all the companies’ fundamental IT policies and security. In the scope of the user, they might see local solutions to help them improve, but depending on the IT policies, they don’t know if the SAP business end users are aware that some ideas are creating potential challenges in security, data harmonization, risks, and costs.

In SAP, we built our SAP Business AI based on three core principles: Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible.

Relevant: AI is embedded directly into SAP's leading enterprise applications and business processes, providing contextually appropriate capabilities where users work daily.

Reliable: AI is grounded in unique business data and business process context, ensuring accurate, trustworthy outputs based on the most important enterprise data with intact business context.

Responsible: AI is delivered with the highest levels of concern for security, privacy, compliance, and ethics, maintaining human oversight and agency while addressing bias and ensuring transparency.

I know these core principles might sound philosophical, but they were designed to help our customers implement SAP Business AI without adding risks to their business. So why not look at anything SAP delivered out of the box first, which was built with these core principles in mind to protect your current investments.

So in this blog, I would like to expose you to where to look at all available capabilities and understand if these capabilities are included in your current Cloud Subscription or not, and also understand how you can move forward with them. Now, I recognize there are many blogs on some of the content already available, which I also recommend you read, e.g. SAP Business AI Catalog and AI Estimator in SAP Discovery Center , Unlock Base AI Package for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Enjoy Joule and Embedded AI Features for Free, etc. However, my focus here is exploring AI use cases, understanding entitlements, and deciding how to move quickly to explore.

To run this case, let’s say you are an SAP Business Suite customer running a couple of SAP Public Cloud Solutions productively, including: SAP Cloud ERP (also known as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition), SAP Integrated Business Planning, Concur Expense, and Concur Travel. Now you want to explore what SAP provides for artificial intelligence included in your cloud subscription.

So first you need to go to the SAP Discovery Center: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-catalog/

SAP Discovery Center.

You can log in to your account if you have one. In the filter section, you will look for the commercial Types : Premium and Base.

Premium: Premium AI includes advanced and specialized AI services powered by AI Units. It enables more complex and scalable use cases such as Joule Premium and Document Grounding, helping organizations extend AI into higher-value scenarios across departments and workflows. In general, if you have not subscribed to these capabilities before, you will be able to add them to your contract with an additional pricing item.

Base: Base AI provides the foundational AI capabilities included in all SAP Cloud applications. These everyday AI features support core business processes—for example, generating insights, recommendations, or automating simple tasks across SAP solutions. Base AI is included in standard cloud subscriptions and can be used without limits or additional cost. So, in other words, you have the rights to use these capabilities as part of your SAP Cloud Subscription. You might need to add a subscription (e.g., Joule Base); however, this subscription comes at no additional cost.

So let's look at all Base capabilities for the system landscape (we are filtering the Discovery Center based on the date of release of this blog, so be aware more capabilities might have been included).

For SAP Cloud ERP:

SAP Discovery Center – Filtered by SAP Cloud ERP and Base commercial type.

For SAP Integrated Business Planning:

SAP Discovery Center – Filtered by SAP Cloud ERP and Base commercial type.

For SAP Concur Travel and Concur Expense:

SAP Discovery Center – Filtered by Concur Travel and Concur Expense and Base commercial type.

So, putting it all together for our customer example (SAP Cloud ERP, SAP IBP, and Concur Travel and Expense), you have almost 40 use cases where you can enable AI within your current system landscape, with entitlements already included in your cloud subscriptions.

SAP Discovery Center – Filtered by SAP Cloud ERP, SAP IBP, Concur Travel and Concur Expense and Base commercial type.

So as indicated, you now have plenty of cases that you can explore with your SAP Business end users that were developed with the core principles that I discussed before: Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible.

Now, when you start exploring the AI capabilities, we have included all resources for you to see what is included, benefits, business value, resources for activation, other related products, and roadmaps.

This is important to review in order to provide your SAP Business end users with a clear explanation of what this capability will bring, along with the fact that since you are running a public cloud solution, you are technically ready to consume the capability out of the box or by just executing an activation.

SAP Discovery Center – Details on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edion, Joule (Base)

Agentic AI

As all industries are moving more toward autonomous agent capabilities to help drive the business, you can also use the Discovery Center to review what SAP has in store. Before you start with development of your own agent-specific capabilities, as indicated above, SAP Agents are also built with the core principles: Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible. So, before you embark on a development cycle of agentic capabilities using other tools in your landscape, I advise you to see what SAP offers out of the box.

SAP Discovery Center – AI Agents for SAP Cloud ERP, SAP IBP, Concur Travel & Concur Expense (Base & Premium).

Keep in mind that SAP Business AI capabilities are expanding quickly, so I recommend you check the Discovery Center regularly.

If you are an SAP Cloud ERP customer, I also suggest you look at this blog as it also presents some principles on AI that are relevant: Foundational Elements for Harnessing the Power of Business AI in Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Ed.

I encourage you to discuss these points with your SAP Customer Success Manager, SAP Account Executive, or SAP Partner. They may be able to provide you with additional recommendations, guidance, and help you discover new ideas on SAP Business AI capabilities that can help you bring more business outcomes.

Your insights and opinions are valuable, so please don't hesitate to contribute by commenting on this blog.

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA or follow @Marco_Valencia (SAP Business Suite Customer Success Management).

entry.title: Poland e-invoicing: SAP Concur Expense with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
entry.content:
Poland e-invoicing: SAP Concur Expense with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition

Poland’s National e‑Invoicing System (KSeF) requires businesses to receive B2B invoices in a structured electronic format. To support this legal requirement for employee expenses, SAP Concur Expense integrates with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

The integration provides a compliant, automated, and user‑friendly way to receive Polish e‑invoices from KSeF and convert eligible invoices into employee expense items.

This document explains how the solution works and provides a high‑level overview of the required configuration.

Solution Overview

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition connects directly to the Polish tax authority platform KSeF (National e‑Invoice System) and retrieves electronic invoices issued to the company. It then:

  • Identifies Travel & Expense (T&E) invoices
  • Routes eligible invoices automatically to SAP Concur Expense

SAP Concur Expense then:

  • Identifies Concur user from employee information in invoice
  • Creates expense items with pre‑populated invoice data and link the invoice retrieved from KSeF to the expense created.

The solution uses employee information provided in Podmiot 3 – Role 11, as recommended by Polish authorities. Employee information in Podmiot 3, Role 11 is used to:

  1. Identify Travel & Expense (T&E) invoices
  2. Find relevant Concur user using Employee Email ID or Employee ID to create expense for the user.

This ensures accurate routing and assignment of expenses without manual intervention.

Business Benefits

  • Supports Poland’s KSeF e‑Invoicing mandate with native invoice retrieval and compliant document handling.
  • Employee‑related invoices are automatically identified and routed using employee information in Podmiot 3 – Role 11, eliminating manual classification.
  • Expense items are created automatically with pre‑populated data, eliminating need of manual expense creation.
  • Direct use of structured e‑invoice data minimizes errors and ensures consistent expense data.
  • Each expense retains the official KSeF invoice reference, supporting tax audits and compliance reporting.
  • Leverages SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition as a central compliance hub, helping organizations adapt to future e‑invoicing regulations.

Configuration Overview and Setup Guidance

The setup requires configuration in both SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and SAP Concur Expense.

1. Set Up Connectivity Between SAP Concur Expense and SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

  • Perform the initial steps in SAP BTP to provision SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.
  • Ensure you have an existing SAP Concur Expense account with the relevant administrator permissions.
  • Establish the connection between SAP Concur Expense account and SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition using the SAP Concur App Center.

 

Setting Up Connectivity to SAP Concur Expense (Country/Region Specifics)
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/setting-up-connectivity-concur-expense-country-region-specifics

2. Configure Poland Process in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition

Activate and configure the Poland‑specific inbound e‑invoicing process in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

Configuring a Process Delivered by the Cloud Edition
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/configuring-process-delivered-by-cloud-edition

As part of this step:

  • Configure routing rules based on Party Role Code ‘11’
  • Maintain the Concur destination (created during connectivity setup) as the target business system

 

Additional details on country‑specific rule parameters are available here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/country-region-specific-parameters-for-rule-conditions

3. Configure Expense Group in SAP Concur Expense

  • Enable Poland as a digital compliance country
  • Assign the configuration at the appropriate Expense Group level

 


Configuration – Expense | SAP Help Portal
https://help.sap.com/docs/CONCUR_EXPENSE

End User Experience:

Employees can see an expense created automatically under Available Expenses with pre‑populated data, including:

  • Invoice ID
  • Merchant Tax ID
  • Government Invoice ID (KSeF ID)
  • The original KSeF e‑invoice and a human‑readable PDF linked to the expense

This significantly reduces effort for employees while ensuring data accuracy and compliance.

Summary

By integrating SAP Concur Expense with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition, organizations can meet Poland’s KSeF requirements while simplifying employee expense processing.

entry.title: Purchase Card Integration with SAP Concur
entry.content:
Introduction: SAP Concur seamlessly integrates purchasing cards (P‑cards) with expense management by automatically importing and categorizing transactions. This automation simplifies expense review, reconciliation, and approval while enhancing visibility into spending trends for better data analysis and vendor negotiations. The system enforces policy rules, manages workflows, and aligns invoices with pre-approved purchases to ensure accuracy and compliance.

Follow the below steps to integrate Purchase Card with SAP Concur:

Step-1: Create the Purchase Card Policy

Navigate to Administration > Expense > Policies. Select any existing policy and click on “Copy Policy” to create new policy

Fill in all the required details to configure the policy. Review all the information entered and click on “Save” to create the Purchase card Policy.

Step 2: Configure Purchase Card Feed Import Definition

  • To integrate purchase card with SAP Concur, a Partner case must be raised with Concur for the feed setup.
  • Once the purchase card integration setup is completed, all purchase cards will be mapped automatically to the employees.

Step 3: Review Purchase Cards mapping to the Card Program

Ensure each purchase card account is correctly linked to its corresponding card program. Proper association enables accurate expense tracking, smoother reconciliation, and more reliable reporting.

Only users with the Company Card Administrator role have permission to assign individual purchasing card accounts to a program.

  1. Navigate to Administration > Company > Tools > Company Card > Manage Accounts.
  2. Search for purchase card accounts by using the Search in options such as Name on Card, Employee Name, or Employee ID as same as corporate cards

     

 

 

      3. Select the desired row and choose Modify to open the Modify Card Account dialog box.

 

      4. Select the appropriate Card Program Name (required).

      5. Click Save.

Step 4: Review Merchant Codes to Expense Types Mappings

Ensure all merchant category codes (MCCs) are correctly mapped to expense types per Purchase Card policy for accurate classification and compliance.

To review and update merchant code mappings:

  1. Go to Administration > Company > Tools > Company Card > Merchant Code Mapping.

       2. Verify all MCCs are present and correctly labelled, mapped to the right expense types.

       3. For each expense type, check its status:

  • If mapped, select it, click Modify to verify or add MCCs via Map New.
  • If Not Mapped, select it, click Modify > Map New, map respective merchant code.       

       4. To configure MCC settings, select an expense type, click Modify, choose the MCC, and click Modify again.

       5. Review Expense Type & merchant code mappings and click “Next” to continue.

    6. Review the mappings, make any necessary adjustments, and then click Done.

Step 5: Validate Purchase Card Settings

Review the purchase card settings:

  1. Navigate to Administration > Company > Tools > Company Card.
  2. Select the Settings tab.

      3. Modify the settings accordingly.

       4. Click on “Save”

Benefits of Using Purchase Card Integration with SAP Concur

  1. Automatic Data Feed: P‑Card transactions are automatically imported from the bank into Concur, eliminating manual expense entry and reducing user errors.
  2. Real-Time Financial Insight: Finance teams get real‑time insight into purchasing activity across departments, vendors, and cost centers through dashboards and reports.
  3. Stronger Policy Compliance: Concur enforces expense rules (MCC restrictions, limits, receipt requirements) at transaction level, reducing non‑compliant spending.
  4. Reduced Invoice Processing Costs: P‑Cards minimize low‑value invoices and AP workload by shifting spend directly to card transactions.

General FAQs:

Q1. What is a Purchase Card (P-Card) in SAP Concur?

A:  A company-issued payment card for purchases, with transactions automatically imported into SAP Concur for employee review and processing.

Q2. Can employees delete P-Card transactions?

A:  No, transactions cannot be deleted but can be reconciled, flagged, commented on, or returned based on permissions.

Q3. What if an expense report is submitted without all P-Card transactions assigned?

A:  Concur may block submission or alert users; unassigned transactions remain in Available Expenses until reconciled.

Q4. How does SAP Concur ensure P-Card compliance?

A: By enforcing policies through rules, audits, receipt requirements, and approval workflows before reimbursement or reconciliation.

entry.title: SAP Concur Integration: Follow SAP Activate Roadmap for Your Cloud Transformation
entry.content:
For customers upgrading or migrating their SAP Concur integration (SAP ICS)—whether from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA or from SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud (RISE) to Public Cloud (GROW)—following the SAP Activate Roadmap. is strongly recommended.

SAP Activate provides standardized, solution‑specific guidance for planning and executing ERP transformations, including integration‑relevant activities and quality gates. The SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer is available via SAP for Me and helps align SAP Concur Integration decisions with the overall S/4HANA migration approach. 

As part of SAP Activate, customers should rely on Fit‑to‑Standard and integration workshops to:

  • Validate existing on-premise‑based integration patterns
  • Align with supported SAP ICS capabilities in S/4HANA
  • Understand differences between Private Cloud and Public Cloud integration models


Official Documentation and SAP Notes

During both planning and execution, we strongly recommend relying on official SAP documentation to ensure a consistent, supported approach.
SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer 

The SAP Activate Roadmap provides structured, solution‑specific guidance, including relevant activities and quality gates. Recommended roadmaps include:

  • SAP Activate for SAP Concur
  • SAP Activate for SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition
  • SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Integration with Concur Solutions for S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Help Portal Documentation for SAP Integration with Concur Solutions 

Key Documentation and SAP Notes for SAP Concur Integration

    • 3140635 – Release Information: SAP S/4HANA Cloud and the Concur Integration
    • 2922806 – FAQ: General Information about the Concur Integration
    • 2924469 – FAQ: Concur Integration (S/4HANA Cloud)
    • 3483312 – SAP Concur Integration: Transitioning from SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA
                             to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Need Help?

If you’re looking for guidance on SAP Concur integration (SAP ICS) as part of your cloud transformation journey, our integration team is here to support you: sapconcurintegration@sap.com

entry.title: AI across Customer Project processes
entry.content:
AI embedded in Cloud ERP systems helps simplify this process. Instead of working with disconnected tools and manual tasks, teams can manage the entire customer project lifecycle in one environment. AI supports decision-making, automates routine activities, and provides insights throughout the process—from opportunity creation to project monitoring and closure. 

That’s why AI across end-to-end processes drives better results for your business. 

But what does that look like in practice? Check out this video, which walks through how SAP helps teams manage customer projects from opportunity to delivery. 

What's on this page 

  1. From Lead to Opportunity in Seconds 
  2. Automated Project Setup 
  3. AI-Powered Staffing 
  4. Track Time smarter 
  5. Intelligent Billing & Profit Clarity 
  6. Performance Monitoring & Closure 

From Lead to Opportunity in Seconds 

The process starts with identifying and qualifying the right lead. The Deal Qualification Assistant enriches lead data, highlights buying signals, and keeps the pipeline updated in the background. 

Then, based on customer activity, the AI Assistant evaluates engagement and turns the lead into an opportunity within seconds. This reduces manual work for teams, while providing more insights and freeing up time for other tasks. 

Once the opportunity is created, Guided Selling Assistant recommends the next actions, flag missing information, and keep the deal on track. 

 After customer discussions, the quotation process begins. AI helps generate proposal communication and supports bid estimate preparation based on project requirements and historical project data. The estimate is then used to build the final proposal and move the deal toward closure. 

Automated Project Setup 

After the deal is confirmed, the project needs to be created in the system. 

With the help of The Project Setup agent, the project manager can create a new project within seconds. The manager simply prompts the agent to create a project and provides basic information of the Project. The agent automatically generates the project structure using predefined best practices. 

AI-Powered Staffing  

Once the project is created, the next step is to assign the right team members. 

The Advanced Skill Match capability analyzes employee skills, experience, and availability to identify the most suitable resources for the project. The project manager can review and compare suggested candidates and select the best fit for the required roles. 

This helps ensure that the project team is composed of specialists who match the project requirements.

Track Time smarter 

Accurate time and expense tracking are essential for project transparency. 

Instead of manually checking for missing entries, the Resource Manager can ask Joule to identify employees who have not recorded their working hours. Joule quickly returns a list of missing time entries and sends reminders to the relevant employees. 

This process takes only a few seconds, helping maintain healthy profitability and prevent revenue leakage. 

Intelligent Billing & Profit Clarity 

SAP Cloud ERP delivers precise, transparent billing across the entire project lifecycle, helping businesses protect project profitability and accelerate cash flow. 

During the pre-billing phase, teams can leave Smart Notes for clients and leads, providing full context behind each billed activity and reducing time spent on clarifications. 

From there, AI supports billing calculations based on rates, resource allocation, and workload, reducing manual effort and keeping the process faster and fully transparent. At the billing-closure stage, if any issues arise, Joule Assistant quickly resolves discrepancies and ensures accurate posting, so final invoices are sent without delays and payments arrive faster. 

Performance Monitoring & Closure 

Throughout the project, the manager monitors progress, financial status, and operational activities. 

AI-supported analytics in the Project Profitability Overview help track overall performance and provide actionable insights. Once all project activities are complete and results are validated, the manager wraps up the project efficiently, with transparent results and maximized profit.

entry.title: Shape What’s Next: SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2026 Is Now Open
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that Cycle 2 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is now live!
This cycle features 38 new projects, offering SAP customers and partners the opportunity to gain early insights into upcoming product developments and collaborate directly with SAP teams to shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

🗓Registration is open until June 19, 2026.

In a rapidly evolving business environment, early involvement can make a meaningful difference. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can work directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing your feedback on concepts, prototypes, and plans while they are still in the early stages.

To help you explore all available opportunities, we’ve prepared a complete overview of the projects.
View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the topics most relevant to you.

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-Assisted Error Remediation for SAP Application Interface Framework
  • AI assisted guidance in the UI theme designer
  • BTP Specific Data Privacy & AI based Data Masking
  • Evolution of SAP BTP Runtimes
  • Fulfillment of business needs through Business Adaption Plans and Solution Implementation
  • Improving the Experience on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime
  • Partner Perspectives in SAP BTP and AI development

Cloud ERP

  • Accelerate Integrations with AI powered SAP Integration Suite
  • Agentic AI in SAP S/4HANA Procurement (Private Cloud Edition)
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI in SAP GRC for SAP HANA
  • AI-Powered Warranty Verification – Co-Innovate the future of service, repair & claims
  • Finance Intelligence on SAP Business Data Cloud
  • Intelligent Returns: Process Discovery & Automation Roadmap
  • Modernization Approaches in Purchase Order (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition)
  • Parallel Unit of Measure

SAP Concur

  • Concur Invoice - AI-first, Reimagine Invoice Experience
  • Seamless configuration promotion for SAP Concur

SAP Globalization

  • AI for UI: Exploring the Usage of AI in Translating SAP's Product User Interface
  • Assessing Demand for AI Speech Services in SAP Translation Hub
  • PayTo Decoded: Let's Build It Together

Customer Success & Delivery

  • Improve SAP Cloud ALM Configuration & Security Analysis for SAP BTP and Cloud

Data & Analytics

  • SAP HANA Cloud Global Landscape Management
  • SAP HANA Cloud Intelligent Advisors
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

Lifecycle Management

  • Comprehensive Planning Assistant for Maintenance Planner
  • New version of SAP Add-On Assembly Kit

SAP Customer Engagement Initiative meets SAP Integration Experience Feedback

  • Hands-on: AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Mgmt - Provisioning & Setup
  • Hands-on Experience of AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Management
  • Hands-on System Experience of the AI-Assisted Process Modeler in SAP Signavio

SAP Learning

  • Reimagining SAP Certification: Evolving a Skills-Based and Future-Ready Certification Experience

Others

  • Autonomous Supply Chain: Embodied AI in the Warehouse
  • Embodied AI Autonomous Asset Inspection
  • Embodied AI: Autonomous Visual Inspection for Quality and Safety
  • Iterate together on the User Experience for Joule Work
  • Providing feedback on the Discovery domain in SAP LeanIX
  • SAP Traceability Hub Repository
  • Support in Flow – Shaping Built-In Support Experience

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Get Early Insights to SAP AI Innovations: Join SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 by June 19
entry.content:
At SAP Sapphire, SAP reaffirmed its strong commitment to AI-driven innovation and its role in transforming businesses.

Innovation at SAP is built on collaboration. Leveraging cutting-edge technologies like Artificial Intelligence, SAP continues to drive meaningful business value for customers. Now, we invite you to be part of this journey by participating in SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects focused on AI.

🗓Registration is open until June 19, 2026.

In today’s fast-evolving business landscape, early involvement is key. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can collaborate directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing feedback on concepts, prototypes, and innovations while they are still taking shape.

View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the selected exciting opportunities:

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-Assisted Error Remediation for SAP Application Interface Framework
  • AI assisted guidance in the UI theme designer
  • BTP Specific Data Privacy & AI based Data Masking
  • Fulfillment of business needs through Business Adaption Plans and Solution Implementation
  • Partner Perspectives in SAP BTP and AI development

Cloud ERP

  • Accelerate Integrations with AI powered SAP Integration Suite
  • Agentic AI in SAP S/4HANA Procurement (Private Cloud Edition)
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI in SAP GRC for SAP HANA
  • AI-Powered Warranty Verification – Co-Innovate the future of service, repair & claims
  • Intelligent Returns: Process Discovery & Automation Roadmap
  • Modernization Approaches in Purchase Order (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition)

SAP Globalization

  • AI for UI: Exploring the Usage of AI in Translating SAP's Product User Interface
  • Assessing Demand for AI Speech Services in SAP Translation Hub

SAP Concur

  • Concur Invoice - AI-first, Reimagine Invoice Experience

SAP Customer Engagement Initiative meets SAP Integration Experience Feedback

  • Hands-on: AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Mgmt - Provisioning & Setup
  • Hands-on Experience of AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Management
  • Hands-on System Experience of the AI-Assisted Process Modeler in SAP Signavio

Others

  • Autonomous Supply Chain: Embodied AI in the Warehouse
  • Embodied AI Autonomous Asset Inspection
  • Embodied AI: Autonomous Visual Inspection for Quality and Safety
  • Iterate together on the User Experience for Joule Work
  • Providing feedback on the Discovery domain in SAP LeanIX

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Guia para acessar o suporte de SAP Concur
entry.content:
Neste blog você terá apresenta uma visão geral das opções de suporte disponíveis no de SAP Concur.  

Usuários Finais 

Se você é um usuário do SAP Concur e precisa de suporte, existem algumas opções disponíveis:  

User Support Desk (USD):  
Este é um serviço pago do SAP Concur que muitas empresas contratam para receber suporte técnico de primeiro nível. Você pode entrar em contato com o User Support Desk por telefone, chat ou abrindo um ticket de ajuda. As opções de contato podem ser encontradas no menu “Help”, acessado pelo ícone “?” no canto superior direito da página inicial do SAP Concur. Caso a opção “Contact Support” não apareça no menu de ajuda, isso indica que sua empresa não possui o serviço User Support Desk.   

  1. Recursos Internos:  
    São recursos disponibilizados pela sua empresa, normalmente na forma de um sistema interno de tickets, um número de telefone dedicado ou um endereço de e-mail específico para suporte. Muitas empresas utilizam a seção “Company Notes” na página inicial do SAP Concur para divulgar essas opções de ajuda internas.  
    Caso sua empresa não tenha disponibilizado informações de contato para suporte, recomendamos entrar em contato com seu gestor direto. Se ele não puder ajudar, tente falar com o administrador interno do SAP Concur da sua empresa, que geralmente faz parte das equipes de Financeiro, Contabilidade, Folha de Pagamento, RH ou TI.  
  2. SAP Concur Community – Suporte e FAQs:  
    Na comunidade SAP Concur, a seção de Suporte e FAQs reúne soluções para os problemas mais comuns enfrentados pelos usuários do sistema. Há um campo de busca onde você pode digitar palavras-chave ou frases para encontrar rapidamente respostas para sua dúvida.  
  3. Fóruns da Comunidade SAP Concur:  
    A comunidade SAP Concur possui fóruns de produtos onde os usuários podem publicar perguntas e receber respostas de outros membros da comunidade ou de colaboradores do SAP Concur. Você também pode pesquisar para verificar se sua dúvida já foi respondida anteriormente. Caso não encontre o que procura, pode criar uma publicação com sua pergunta.  
    Observação: é necessário ser um membro registrado e estar logado na comunidade SAP Concur para publicar nos fóruns.  
  4. SAP Help Portal:  
    Pode ser acessado diretamente dentro do SAP Concur pelo menu “Help”. Para acessá-lo, clique no ícone “?” no canto superior direito da tela. Você verá uma lista de opções de ajuda com base nos produtos utilizados pela sua empresa.   

Se o seu problema estiver relacionado ao acesso ao SAP Concur, consulte os artigos abaixo:  

  • How Do I Sign In to My SAP Concur Profile? - SAP Concur Community 
  • Why Can't I Sign In to the SAP Concur Tool? - SAP Concur Community 

Administradores  

Se você é um Authorized Support Contact (ASC) da sua empresa ou um administrador responsável por realizar alterações no sistema SAP Concur, faça login no seu perfil SAP Concur, clique no ícone “?” no canto superior direito e selecione “Contact Support” no menu de ajuda.  

Isso permitirá que você:  

  • converse via chat com um representante;   
  • abra um chamado;   
  • ou ligue para o suporte por telefone.   

O número de suporte deve ser utilizado apenas para problemas críticos de prioridade P1 (indisponibilidade do sistema).  

Um problema P1 ou crítico é definido como qualquer situação que cause impactos graves na execução de processos essenciais de negócio ou de TI, sem solução alternativa disponível. Esse tipo de incidente exige atenção imediata porque afeta todos os usuários.  

Exemplos de problemas P1 incluem, mas não se limitam à impossibilidade de:  

  • reservar viagens;   
  • criar relatórios de despesas;   
  • enviar solicitações de viagem;   
  • enviar solicitações de fatura;   
  • acessar o SAP Concur.   

Caso você tenha um problema P1, pode ligar para +1 855-895-4815 e selecionar a opção “Authorized Support Contact” no menu.  

Recursos adicionais para Authorized Support Contacts (ASC)  

O papel de Authorized Support Contact é uma permissão atribuída a determinadas pessoas dentro da organização. Recomenda-se que não haja mais de cinco pessoas designadas como ASC.  

Como ASC, você possui acesso ampliado ao suporte e aos recursos do SAP Concur, incluindo:  

  • suporte técnico através do SAP Concur Support Portal;   
  • atendimento telefônico pelo número +1 855-895-4815 (somente para problemas P1);   
  • esclarecimento de dúvidas sobre faturamento através do Online Billing Portal Guide.  

Obrigada pela leitura!
